notes:
Thus far I have not yet had any real luck running EverQuest (Steam variant) in Proton, but for the first time today (with Proton 4.2-3) it actually ran!  The launcher/patcher loaded fine and patched everything, and then the game itself loaded as far as the character select screen (which is where I gave up on it).  The problems (for me currently) are that there were no graphics visible for the characters, nor for the game UI (although some text from the UI displayed, the graphical bits did not).  I will try later to do some hackery using ProtonTricks, etc. and see if I can improve on things any.

In the meantime, anyone who's really dead-set on playing this game might wish to try the download version from the EverQuest website in PlayOnLinux, Lutris, or even just plain old vanilla WINE (latest version you can get), as this game runs nearly native for me in WINE.  Once you get it running, you can always add it to Steam as an external game for easy launching and access to some of the basic Steam features.
title: EverQuest

title: EverQuest

cpu: AMD A10-7850K APU with Radeon R7 Graphics
notes: I'm able to update the game after it installs, and it actually gets in game, but you can't see the characters at the player select screen. I see the background, some firey torches, and butterlies flying around, but that's it.
title: EverQuest

notes:
Had to install d3dx9_43 and corefonts via protontricks (https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks) this solved missing graphics (d3dx9_43), and slow performance (corefonts).

Runs better than Wine, handles switching between windowed and full screen better. No issues with mouse alignment as per Wine.

Only tested in group /  solo setting so far. Has crashed once when switching from Windowed to Full screen.
title: EverQuest

notes:
In your eqclient.ini you have to edit the line "VertexShaders=TRUE" to "VertexShaders=FALSE", in order for your player model to show up. You also need to use protontricks and install two things. d3dx9_43, and core fonts. The proper way to use protontricks is "./protontricks 205710 d3dx9_43", and "./protontricks 205710 corefonts" . 205710 being the app ID for everquest F2P on steam. For people having issues with VERY slow loading times and connection time outs, you have to edit your eqclient.ini with these lines. 
CPUAffinity0=-1
CPUAffinity1=-1
CPUAffinity2=-1
CPUAffinity3=-1
Since i have a quad core processor, it might be different for people with more. So the game was running great, however they had a patch, and something changed in the games UI, which for some reason causes the game to throw a error window when you press "play everquest" in the server window.
title: EverQuest
